item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh. The first time I read this over I had this whole wtf feeling that the absurdity level of an irregular haircut instantly giving you irregular powers was a bit too much. Then I see the positive reception on this discussion board, and you know what, SCPs are meant to be weird just as much as they are meant to be scary (or funny) depending on the circumstances. And in retrospect, the evolution of the D-Class in controlling his hair in such an advanced way is fun.

I'm interested to see a debate being brought about this hairstyle as to whether Safe is sufficient (in universe, or maybe out as well). I technically agree that it's enough given that the instructions are far too specific and can't necessarily be accidentally simply just made. Euclid would have been the case only if a few existing notable reports have been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It gets progressively weirder and more intriguing without ever going over the top. Very controlled exposition. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so weird, I love it!
It's another one of those concepts that sounds so stupid (but eye-catching) when you see the name "Anomalous Hairstyle", but is so good.

It's like they become more insect-like (like how flies and other bugs have "hairs" that help them stick to surfaces) the longer the haircut is maintained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if it should be classified as "Safe" since anyone can technically have it. I get it would be very unlikely due to the strange and precise procedure but it's still a possibility.

But still the thought that there are anomalies that ANYONE could have access to and not know it? A universal SCP? That's pretty awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice! A well written object with strange vibes all over it.

I noticed that you only allow testing of the haircut on D-Class, and that seems a bit odd. Wouldn't a researcher possibly want to record what having the haircut would be like? On top of that, D-Class can (and in fact DID) try to use this to escape, so it seems wrong to give them the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm doing the haircut. Anyone want pictures of my dancing hair?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This must be the work of an enemy Stand!
(Somebody had to say it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I could think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The 100th upvote!
Ive read many SPCs where people gained strange powers, but this idea has never been explored before. Though it wasn't particularly threatning, thought provoking, or disturbing, it was reasonably interesting from a biological perspective.

Oh, and at the beginning, I thought this was going to be a joke to explain how some people seemed to have perfect hair all the time. That would have been pretty funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like Scorpion said, it's a completely absurd idea taken very seriously. It's not exactly funny, but it is sort of grin-inducing.

Very good show! Here, have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat… Prehensile hair has always sounded useful to me and the test log is well written so; Upvoted.
Also one of the name drops of the procedure called it gamma-2499 rather than gamma-2944, like the rest were, so I changed it. Hopefully not out of line, and if it is reverted, it's in the test log at 74 days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If only I could upvote comments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like that magical hair tonic episode from Billy and Mandy combined with Love Deluxe from JoJo. Guys with his kind of haircut basically turn into Bigfoot with prehensile hair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was actually very interesting. My only nitpick was the absence of a concrete time structure used in the testing log. I feel that it would really improve with maybe a table or some dates, along with the "days since initial haircut" counter.

Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Several hours after expiration, D-2944-17's hair follicles also entered rigor mortis like state, suspending his body approximately an inch off of the operating table.

thats some pre intro fringe© scene right there.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was very well-done, I particularly enjoyed the test log. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I concur with "An Anomalous Haircut" as the perfect title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/)^.^/)+1

I was just keeping this upvote warm for you. With my own body hair. It's not creepy.

You know, I would honestly just stick with "An Anomalous Haircut"; it may seem plain, but it's descriptive and I can guarantee you that people are going to click on it when they see it.

Oh, and congrats on the rocket-fast jump to +40. Aaand now it's +41.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, thanks for all that! Yor irks ha been fixd. That, too many, commas, part, I did some deleting on, is that, like, better, now? Or still moar? I've changed the mainlist name to "An Anomalous Haircut", but that's still up for change. Any good names? I've got: "The Panamanian Pompadour", "The Canal Cut", and "A man, a plan, and a barbershop". I'm not really feeling any of these, got anythin' good?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2944 is an anomalous hairstyle

As of that line, pretty much the only thing that could have stopped me from upvoting was for the rest to devolve into illegible creepypasta. All the better that the testing logs were every bit as fun to read. This is a great example of how taking a completely absurd idea completely seriously can be a gold mine, both for SCP's and for speculative fiction in general.

item.title: Re: Well...
item.content:encoded:
Skullet 2.0?

Eskulit vitu, vaguely based on how a spanish speaker would write "Skullet V2" if listened in English.

item.title: Re: Well...
item.content:encoded: It should be in Spanish and probably have nothing obvious to do with that hairstyle. Maybe look at Cuban punk band names, given the history of extremely hardcore (suicidal, give yourself AIDS on purpose, hardcore) countercultural rock there. Or some variation of "Los Frikis".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is more that an intricate series of steps. It is asymmetrical, and genuinely stupid looking.

item.title: Re: Well...
item.content:encoded: Freaky Fro?

item.title: Well...
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone that helped this, seeing it in positives is really weird, and I appreciate all the comments. I sort of got distracted after I posted this, and failed to announce it or edit the mainlist, didn't want to make any mess that just had to be cleaned up in case it fell into the negs. Zacharia H., thanks for that, I'll get on those as soon as I can. I was trying to get a unique idea, with a series 1 sort of vibe, so it's good to know that got across. Again, thanks guys.

Any suggestions for a sweet name for the Mullet 2.0?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw the original anomalous hairstyle draft and thought, hey, this is cute, suddenly getting cartoon tentacle hair is cool.

But then you kept building on it. Went through all the ramifications of what it means to control your follicles. Just how far your hair could matter. And you told a story on top of it, of the D-class with the hairstyle. You answered what does it mean and what do we see with the process.

Upvoted enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It can. It's just generally not used that way. Isn't English weird?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it, love it, love it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't think I would enjoy this article like I did, great job man. +1 for anomalous hairstyles

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if "personnel" can be singular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, although modern trends could accidentally create this hairstyle, pointing out that performing the haircut requires an intrincate series of steps would be enough for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, the hairstyle's back!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really digging the originality, and I'm itching to upvote, but a lot of little issues with the writing just irk me.

Things wot irk me...
hide silly irks

The abilities are proportional to how well the instructions were followed, a subject will have greater awareness and control over their abilities the lower the error in the procedure.

Comma splice; there should be a semicolon or full stop in there.

The phenomenon is also somewhat difficult to study, as the user can no longer control follicles once they fall, or are plucked, out. In addition to being capable of undulating motions, the hair is also less receptive to static buildup.

The second sentence does not logically follow on from the first; so why do they make up an entire paragraph?

Observed behaviors also include several unconscious actions, for example, if threatened, a subject's hair may become rigid and stand on end, all over the body. Additionally, should a subject have need of an item, and reach for it, hair up the arm and on the hand will subtly point towards the desired object.

I feel like there's at least two commas too many in there.

D-2944-17 was capable of easily shrugging off light light blows

I'm assuming the double "light" was just a typing error, but it stands out.

EDIT: I noticed you haven't changed the [ACCESS DENIED] title for this on the mainlist yet! Every fine hairstyle needs a name, you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The instructions seem to make it clear that it would be very difficult to get this awful haircut by accident. Without them I would be wondering why this isn't widespread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Upvoted at:

SCP-2944 is an anomalous hairstyle

The rest of the article did not disappoint! You really fleshed (haired?) this one out, and all the extra thought really shows.

EDIT: I just realized that this is a thing that gives you powers, and you still managed to make it super awesome!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This:

really reminds me of a 1st-series piece in a great way. +1

Although, I still feel that there's no reason to censor the name, and that the instructions aren't neccesary for the article (perhaps as an addendum or suplement?).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great! It's like you spun the "wheel of unused ideas," landed on "anomalous haircut", and made an article that really reminds me of a 1st-series piece in a great way. +1.
